title: Design an Explicit Demo-to-Real-Trip Clone Flow
objective: Prepare an evidence-based architecture analysis only; do not decide or implement the Clone Flow.

knownFacts.claim: Built-in Demo identity is local-only and read-only.
Opening the Demo uses separate React view state and does not add a trip to myTrips or create Firebase data.

evidence.symbol: does not call Firebase, Offline Cache, or myTrips storage while opening the demo

knownFacts.claim: The production Demo Preview hides Clone while preserving a separately tested explicit clone callback contract.

evidence.symbol: clone action visibility and explicit click tests

knownFacts.claim: Current real-trip creation generates a room ID, persists rooms/{roomId}, then adds metadata to myTrips and navigates to the new room.
Clone Flow is deferred and any future clone requires explicit user action plus canonicalization and failure design.
Offline Preview is a separate local, read-only snapshot boundary and not a source for writable cloning.

bindingConstraints: Opening the Demo must not write Firebase, myTrips, or Offline Trip Cache.
Clone and Open Demo must be distinct explicit actions.
No product change is authorized before a proposed decision and Human Approval.
Do not access production Firebase or deploy.
questions: What content and dates should it copy?
What is the safe Firebase and myTrips write order?
How should rollback and duplicate prevention work?
How should Demo IDs, members, tickets, expenses, checklist data, and example-only fields transform?
How should offline, identity, network, navigation, authorization, and testing boundaries behave?

execution.reason: Human Brief preparation only; no Agent has been executed.
